question_text: Which of the following best explains why a balloon expands when you blow air into it?

options.label: A
options.text: The air particles collide with the balloon walls, pushing them outward

options.label: B
options.text: The rubber of the balloon naturally wants to stretch

options.label: C
options.text: The air becomes heavier and pulls the balloon outward

options.label: D
options.text: The balloon expands because air is invisible

explanation: More air particles inside the balloon means more collisions with the rubber walls. These collisions exert an outward force, causing the balloon to expand and stretch.
